html:
[1]

Idiomatic Expressions

  • 庙方四丈，不作墉壁 - Describes the temple's structure, emphasizing its grandeur and simplicity. '庙方四丈' means the temple is four zhang (a traditional Chinese unit of length) on each side, and '不作墉壁' means there are no walls or enclosures.
  • 道广五尺，夹树兰香 - Describes the path leading to the temple, which is five chi wide (another traditional Chinese unit of length) and lined with fragrant orchids.
  • 海中有金台，出水百丈，结构巧丽，穷尽神工，横光岩渚，竦曜星汉 - Describes a golden platform in the sea that rises a hundred zhang above the water, with intricate and beautiful architecture, and radiates light across the rocks and stars.
  • 台内有金几，雕文备置，上有百味之食，四大力神常立守护 - Details the interior of the golden platform, which has a golden table with elaborate carvings, placed with a variety of foods, and guarded by four powerful gods.
  • 有一五通仙人来，欲甘膳，四神排击，延而退 - Tells of a five-way immortal who tried to take the food, but was repelled by the four gods.
  • 邺城凤阳门五层楼，去地二十丈，长四十丈，广二十丈，安金凤皇二头于其上 - Describes a five-story tower in the city of Yecheng, which is twenty zhang high, forty zhang long, and twenty zhang wide, with two golden phoenixes on top.
  • 石季龙将衰，一头飞入漳河，清朗见在水底；一头今犹存 - States that as Shi Jilong's power waned, one of the phoenixes flew into the Zhang River and can still be seen at the bottom of the water, while the other remains on the tower.
  • 始兴县有皋天子国。因山崎岖，十有余里，坑堑数重，阡陌交通 - Describes a kingdom of the Gao Emperor in Shixing County, which is rugged and mountainous, spanning over ten li (a traditional Chinese unit of distance), with multiple trenches and intersecting paths.
  • 城内堂基碎瓦，柱穿犹存 - Mentions the ruins of the city, with broken tiles and columns still standing.
  • 东有皋天子冢 - Refers to the tomb of the Gao Emperor located to the east.
  • 皋天子，未之闻也 - Indicates that the Gao Emperor is not well-known.
  • 始兴县有睪天子城，城东有冢 - Describes a city of the Ge Emperor in Shixing County, with a tomb to the east of the city.
  • 昔有发之者，垂陷，而冢里有角声震于外，惧而塞之 - Tells of someone who tried to open the tomb, but it nearly collapsed, and a sound of horns was heard from inside, causing fear and the tomb being sealed.

Cultural and Historical Allusions

  • 庙 - Refers to a religious or ancestral temple.
  • 墉壁 - Refers to walls or enclosures.
  • 斋者 - Refers to those who are fasting or preparing for a ritual.
  • 浴兰汤 - A traditional Chinese ritual bath using orchids, symbolizing purification.
  • 金台 - A golden platform, often associated with divine or mythical places.
  • 五通仙人 - A five-way immortal, a type of deity in Chinese mythology.
  • 四大力神 - Four powerful gods, often associated with protection and guardianship.
  • 邺城 - The city of Yecheng, an important historical site in China.
  • 凤阳门 - The Phoenix Gate, a significant gate in Yecheng.
  • 石季龙 - Shi Jilong, a historical figure in the Jin Dynasty.
  • 漳河 - The Zhang River, a river in China.
  • 始兴县 - Shixing County, a location in China.
  • 皋天子 - The Gao Emperor, a historical or legendary figure.
  • 睪天子 - The Ge Emperor, another historical or legendary figure.

Proper Nouns

  • 庙 - Temple.
  • 墉壁 - Walls or enclosures.
  • 斋者 - Those who are fasting or preparing for a ritual.
  • 浴兰汤 - Orchid Bath.
  • 金台 - Golden Platform.
  • 五通仙人 - Five-way Immortal.

[2]

Idiomatic Expressions

  • 始興靈水，源有湯泉 - Descriptive phrase setting the scene.
  • 見其上烝氣高數十丈 - Steam rises tens of feet high; '烝氣' specifically refers to steam or vapor.
  • 生物投之，須臾便熟 - Idiom describing the heat of the spring; living things cook quickly.
  • 泉中常有細赤魚出游，莫有獲者 - Descriptive phrase with a hint of mystery; tiny red fish swim in the spring but are not caught.
  • 艾縣輔山有溫冷二泉，同出一山之足 - Descriptive phrase highlighting the unique feature of the springs.
  • 兩泉發源，相去數尺 - Precise measurement; two springs originate a few feet apart.
  • 熱泉可煮雞豚，冰泉常若冰生 - Descriptive phrase emphasizing temperature differences; hot spring can cook chickens and pigs, cold spring is always like ice.
  • 雙流數丈而合，俱會於一溪 - Descriptive phrase describing the flow of the springs.
  • 襄邑縣南瀨鄉，老子之舊鄉也 - Historical reference to the birthplace of Laozi.
  • 有老子廟，廟中有九井，能潔齋入祠者，水溫清隨人意念 - Descriptive phrase highlighting the spiritual significance of the temple.
  • 耒陽縣東北有 - Incomplete phrase, context needed for full understanding.

[3]

Idiomatic Expressions

  • 莫測其深 - Phrase meaning 'its depth cannot be measured,' indicating the golden pool's immeasurable depth.
  • 引之，遂滿一船 - To pull it, and it filled an entire boat, referring to the gold chain being pulled and filling the boat.
  • 聲貌莽壯 - Its sound and appearance were fierce and mighty, describing the imposing presence of the golden bull.
  • 牛因奮勇躍而還潭 - The bull, with great courage, leapt back into the pool, indicating the bull's sudden return to the water.
  • 鎖乃將盡 - The chain was nearly exhausted, meaning the chain was almost entirely pulled out.
  • 以刀斫得數尺 - With a blade, he cut off several feet, indicating the fisherman cut a portion of the chain.
  • 潭、濑因此取名 - The pool and rapid were thus named, indicating the origin of their names.
  • 淮南牛渚津水極深 - The waters of Niu Zhu Jin in Huainan are extremely deep, a geographical description.
  • 以金為鎖絆 - Tethered with a golden chain, describing the golden bull being bound.
  • 自南行十余里 - Traveling south for more than ten li, indicating distance.
  • 冠距如生 - Its comb and spurs were as if alive, describing the lifelike appearance of the stone rooster.
  • 常寶玩之 - Often cherished and played with it, indicating the master's affection for the stone rooster.
  • 吾其終乎 - Is this the sign of my end? A rhetorical question about death.
  • 因與知故诀别 - Thus, he bid farewell to his friends and relatives, indicating a final farewell.
  • 三峰最为竦桀 - The three peaks are the most towering and steep, describing the height and prominence of the peaks.
  • 自非清霁素朝，不可望见 - Unless on a clear morning, one cannot see them, indicating visibility conditions.
  • 分映青林 - Splitting into reflections among the green woods, describing the light and water reflections.
  • 直注山下 - Directly pouring down to the foot of the mountain, describing the waterfall’s direction.
  • 雖纖羅不動 - Though the fine silk is not moving, a metaphor for stillness or calm.
  • 其上翛翛 - Above it, the air is cool and clear, describing the atmosphere.
  • 恒凄清风也 - It is always filled with a chill, clear wind, indicating the constant presence of a cold, fresh breeze.
  • 石數枚，形圓若鏡 - Several stones, round like mirrors, describing the shape of the stones.
  • 明可以鑑人 - Bright enough to reflect a person's image, indicating the reflective quality.
  • 以火燎一枚 - He burned one with fire, indicating the act of setting fire to the stone.
  • 其人眼乃失明 - His eyes then became blind, the consequence of the act.

Cultural and Historical Allusions

  • 巴丘县 - A county in ancient China, likely in modern-day Hunan province.
  • 金冈 - Golden Hill, a geographical feature in the area of Baqiu County.
  • 黄金潭 - Golden Pool, a name derived from the legend of the golden chain.
  • 黄金濑 - Golden Rapid, a name for the river rapid associated with the legend.
  • 淮南牛渚津 - A location in the Huainan region, likely near the Yangtze River, historically known for its deep waters.
  • 庐山 - Lu Mountain, a famous mountain in Jiangxi province, known for its scenic beauty and historical significance in Daoist and Buddhist traditions.
  • 鸡山 - Chicken Mountain, a name associated with the stone rooster legend.
  • 道士李镇 - Daoist priest named Li Zhen, whose story is part of the local folklore.
  • 三峰 - Three Peaks, referring to the most prominent peaks of Lu Mountain.
  • 宫亭湖 - Gongting Lake, a lake near Lu Mountain, historically associated with Daoist legends and natural wonders.
  • 石镜 - Stone Mirror, a term for a naturally polished stone that reflects images, common in Chinese folklore and landscape descriptions.

Proper Nouns

  • 巴丘县 - Baqiu County, a historical county in ancient China, now part of modern-day Hunan.
  • 金冈 - Jin Gang, 'Golden Hill,' a geographical feature near Baqiu County.
  • 黄金潭 - Huangjin Tan, 'Golden Pool,' a name derived from the legend.
  • 黄金濑 - Huangjin La, 'Golden Rapid,' a river rapid associated with the legend.
  • 淮南 - Huainan, a region in eastern China, historically known for its deep waters and natural features.
  • 牛渚津 - Niu Zhi Jin, a location near the Yangtze River, historically associated with deep water and legends.
  • 庐山 - Lu Mountain, a famous mountain in Jiangxi, known for its scenic beauty and historical significance.
  • 鸡山 - Ji Mountain, 'Chicken Mountain,' a name associated with the stone rooster legend.
  • 道士李镇 - Daoist Master Li Zhen, a Daoist priest from the legend.
  • 宫亭湖 - Gongting Lake, a lake near Lu Mountain, historically associated with Daoist legends and natural wonders.
  • 石镜 - Shijing, 'Stone Mirror,' a term for naturally polished stones that reflect images.

[4]

Idiomatic Expressions

  • 請告神 - Formal way of saying 'praying to the god'.
  • 因城為台 - Descriptive phrase indicating the construction method.
  • 聒於耳 - Emphasizes the loud and persistent nature of the sounds.
  • 道逢 - Encountered on the road.
  • 相傳 - It is said.

Cultural and Historical Allusions

  • 山陰縣 - Shanyin County, a historical place in present-day Shaoxing, Zhejiang Province.
  • 九侯神山 - A sacred mountain, possibly referring to a local deity or a legendary site.
  • 魏武帝 - Cao Cao, a powerful warlord and statesman during the late Eastern Han Dynasty.
  • 江都王建 - Liu Jian, a prince of the Han Dynasty, who was buried in Guangling.
  • 廣陵 - Guangling, an ancient name for Yangzhou, a city in Jiangsu Province.
  • 樂安縣 - Le'an County.
  • 平都縣 - Pingdu County.
  • 露白村 - Luobai Village.

Proper Nouns

  • 山陰縣 - Shanyin County
  • 九侯神山 - Sacred Mountain of the Nine Lords
  • 魏武帝 - Cao Cao
  • 江都王建 - Liu Jian
  • 廣陵 - Guangling
  • 樂安縣 - Le'an County
  • 平都縣 - Pingdu County
  • 露白村 - Luobai Village

[5]

Idiomatic Expressions

  • 眩潭 - A pool with a confusing name, possibly a local legend or myth.
  • 大鹏 - A mythical bird in Chinese mythology, often associated with the giant Peng bird from the *Zhuangzi*.

Cultural and Historical Allusions

  • 硕县 - A place name, possibly a county in ancient China.
  • 东莱 - A region in ancient China, known for its spiritual sensitivity.
  • 楚文王 - King Wen of Chu, a historical figure from the Chu state during the Spring and Autumn period.
  • 云梦 - A vast area in the Huai River basin, known for its rich wildlife and hunting grounds.
  • 博物君子 - A knowledgeable person, often referring to scholars or experts.

Proper Nouns

  • 眩潭 - A pool with a confusing name.
  • 东莱 - A region in ancient China.
  • 楚文王 - King Wen of Chu.
  • 云梦 - A vast area in the Huai River basin.
  • 大鹏 - A mythical bird in Chinese mythology.
  • 博物君子 - A knowledgeable person.

[6]

Idiomatic Expressions

  • 微行 - Emperor's incognito travels to avoid formalities.
  • 国色 - Euphemism for exceptional beauty, often applied to women.
  • 留宿 - To stay overnight.
  • 夜与婢□ - Refers to a sexual encounter; '夜' means 'at night,' and '与' means 'with.'
  • 书生亦家宿 - '书生' refers to a scholar or student, and '家宿' means to stay at home.
  • 善天文 - Knowledge of astronomy, highly valued in ancient China.
  • 客星移掩帝座甚逼 - '客星' refers to a temporary star, '移掩帝座' means it moved to cover the emperor's seat, and '甚逼' indicates it was very close.
  • 书生大惊跃 - To be greatly surprised and jump.
  • 连呼咄咄 - Interjection expressing surprise or warning.
  • 不觉声高 - Unconsciously, the voice became loud.
  • 有书生亦家宿 - '书生' refers to a scholar or student, and '家宿' means to stay at home.
  • 操刀将欲入户 - '操刀' means 'holding a knife,' and '将欲入户' means 'about to enter the house.'
  • 闻书生声急 - '闻' means 'to hear,' and '声急' means 'the voice was urgent.'
  • 谓为己故 - '谓为' means 'thought,' and '己故' means 'for my own sake.'
  • 蹙缩走 - To shrink back and run away.
  • 客星应时即退 - '应时' means 'at the right time,' and '即退' means 'immediately retreated.'
  • 帝闻其声，异而召问之 - '异' means 'strange,' and '召问之' means 'called him to ask.'
  • 书生具说所见 - '具说' means 'to fully explain,' and '所见' means 'what he saw.'
  • 乃悟曰 - '乃悟' means 'then realized,' and '曰' means 'said.'
  • 此人是婢婿 - '此人' means 'this person,' and '婢婿' means 'the husband of the maid.'
  • 将欲肆其凶于朕 - '将欲' means 'about to,' '肆其凶' means 'unleash his evil,' and '于朕' means 'on me.'
  • 召羽林 - '召' means 'to summon,' and '羽林' refers to the imperial guards.
  • 语主人曰 - '语' means 'to tell,' and '主人' means 'the host.'
  • 朕，天子也 - '朕' is the first-person pronoun used by the emperor, and '天子' means 'Son of Heaven' or 'emperor.'
  • 擒奴伏诛 - '擒奴' means 'capture the slave,' and '伏诛' means 'to be executed.'
  • 厚赐书生 - '厚赐' means 'to give a generous reward,' and '书生' means 'the scholar.'
  • 汉武见物如牛肝 - '见物' means 'saw something,' and '如牛肝' means 'like a cow's liver.'
  • 入地不动 - '入地' means 'sink into the ground,' and '不动' means 'not moving.'
  • 问东方朔 - '问' means 'to ask,' and '东方朔' is a famous scholar and court jester during the Han dynasty.
  • 惟酒可以忘愁 - '惟' means 'only,' '酒' means 'wine,' and '忘愁' means 'forget sorrow.'
  • 以酒灌之，即消 - '以酒灌之' means 'to pour wine over it,' and '即消' means 'immediately disappeared.'
  • 汉武帝在甘泉宫 - '甘泉宫' is a palace in the Han dynasty.
  • 有玉女降 - '玉女' means 'a beautiful woman,' and '降' means 'to descend' or 'to appear.'
  • 常与帝围棋相娱 - '常' means 'often,' '围棋' means 'Go' (a board game), and '相娱' means 'to entertain each other.'
  • 女风姿端正 - '风姿' means 'graceful appearance,' and '端正' means 'upright and proper.'
  • 帝密悦 - '密' means 'secretly,' and '悦' means 'pleased.'
  • 乃欲逼之 - '乃' means 'then,' '欲' means 'want,' and '逼之' means 'to force her.'
  • 女因唾帝面而去 - '因' means 'because,' '唾' means 'spit,' '面' means 'face,' and '而去' means 'and left.'
  • 遂病疮经年 - '遂' means 'then,' '病疮' means 'developed sores,' and '经年' means 'for a year.'
  • 避暑甘泉宫，正其时也 - '避暑' means 'to escape the heat,' '甘泉宫' is the palace, and '正其时也' means 'it was the right time.'

Cultural and Historical Allusions

  • 汉武帝 - Emperor Wu of Han, one of the most powerful and influential emperors of the Han dynasty.
  • 微行 - Incognito travels, often used by emperors to avoid the formalities of official visits.
  • 国色 - Term used to describe exceptional beauty, often applied to women.
  • 书生 - Scholar or student, often used to describe someone with knowledge and education.
  • 善天文 - Knowledge of astronomy, which was highly valued in ancient China.
  • 客星 - Temporary star, often associated with significant events or omens.
  • 帝座 - Emperor's seat, symbolizing the emperor's position and power.
  • 羽林 - Imperial guards, responsible for protecting the emperor.
  • 东方朔 - Famous scholar and court jester during the Han dynasty, known for his wit and wisdom.
  • 甘泉宫 - Palace in the Han dynasty, often used for official and ceremonial purposes.
  • 玉女 - Beautiful woman, often used in legends and stories to describe a goddess or a celestial being.
  • 围棋 - Board game Go, highly regarded in ancient China and often played by scholars and nobles.

[7]

Idiomatic Expressions

  • 甘泉王母降 - Classical expression meaning 'the Queen Mother of the West descended at Ganquan.'
  • 未央 - Refers to Weiyang Palace, a significant imperial palace in Chang'an during the Han dynasty.
  • 黍臛 - A dish made of millet and meat, often served as a delicacy.
  • 老臣冒死自诉 - A formal expression meaning 'an old subject risks his life to appeal.'
  • 面目赪皱 - Describes a face that is red and wrinkled, indicating old age and possibly illness.
  • 须发皓白 - Describes white beard and hair, further emphasizing the old age of the character.
  • 拄杖偻步 - Describes the act of walking with a cane, bent over, indicating extreme oldness and frailty.
  • 笃老之极 - Means 'extremely old and frail,' reinforcing the description of the old man.
  • 叟姓字何？居在何处？何所病苦，而来诉朕？ - A series of questions asking for the name, residence, and reason for the old man's plea.
  • 缘柱而下 - Means 'climbing down the pillar,' describing the old man's movement.
  • 放杖稽首 - Means 'putting down the cane and bowing deeply,' a gesture of deep respect or submission.
  • 默而不言 - Means 'silent and not speaking,' indicating the old man's initial silence.
  • 仰头视屋，俯指帝脚 - Describes the old man looking up at the ceiling and then pointing down at the emperor's feet.
  • 忽然不见 - Means 'suddenly disappeared,' indicating the sudden vanishing of the old man.
  • 骇愕不知何等 - Means 'shocked and unable to understand what it was,' describing the emperor's reaction.
  • 东方朔必识之 - Means 'Dongfang Shuo must know it,' indicating the emperor's belief that Dongfang Shuo would recognize the old man.
  • 其名为‘藻兼’，水木之精也 - Means 'his name is 'Zaojian,' a spirit of water and wood,' identifying the old man as a spirit.
  • 夏巢幽林，冬潜深河 - Describes the spirit's habitat, living in deep forests in summer and deep rivers in winter.
  • 陛下顷日频兴造宫室，斩伐其居 - Means 'Your Majesty recently frequently built palaces, cutting down their homes,' explaining the reason for the old man's plea.
  • 愿陛下宫室足于此也 - Means 'I hope Your Majesty's palaces are sufficient here,' expressing the spirit's wish for the emperor to stop building.
  • 瓠子河 - Refers to the Huizi River, a river in ancient China.
  • 水底有弦歌之声 - Means 'there was a sound of stringed music and singing under the water,' describing the mysterious music heard.
  • 前梁上翁及年少数人 - Means 'the old man on the beam and several young people,' describing the appearance of the spirits.
  • 绛衣素带，缨佩甚鲜 - Describes the attire of the young people, with red clothes, white belts, and bright decorations.
  • 皆长八九寸，有一人，长尺余 - Describes the size of the spirits, with most being about 8-9 inches tall and one being over a foot tall.
  • 凌波而出，衣不沾濡 - Means 'emerging from the water, their clothes not wet,' describing the spirits' appearance.
  • 或有挟乐器者 - Means 'some were holding musical instruments,' indicating the presence of instruments.
  • 为之辍膳 - Means 'caused him to stop eating,' describing the emperor's reaction to the music.
  • 命列坐于食案前 - Means 'ordered them to sit in a row in front of the dining table,' describing the emperor's command.

Cultural and Historical Allusions

  • 甘泉 - Refers to a palace or temple in Chang'an, likely a significant location during the Han dynasty.
  • 未央 - Refers to Weiyang Palace, a major imperial palace in Chang'an.
  • 黍臛 - A traditional dish made of millet and meat, often served as a delicacy.
  • 王母 - The Queen Mother of the West, a figure in Chinese mythology known for her immortality and supernatural powers.
  • 东方朔 - A famous scholar and court jester during the Han dynasty, known for his wit and wisdom.
  • 瓠子河 - The Huizi River, a river in ancient China, often associated with historical and mythological events.
  • 弦歌之声 - Refers to the sound of stringed instruments and singing, often associated with musical performances or spiritual activities.

Proper Nouns

  • 甘泉 - A palace or temple in Chang'an.
  • 未央 - Weiyang Palace, a major imperial palace in Chang'an.
  • 黍臛 - A traditional dish made of millet and meat.
  • 王母 - The Queen Mother of the West, a figure in Chinese mythology.
  • 东方朔 - A famous scholar and court jester during the Han dynasty.
  • 瓠子河 - The Huizi River, a river in ancient China.
  • 弦歌之声 - The sound of stringed instruments and singing.

[8]

Idiomatic Expressions

  • 聞水底奏樂，為是君耶 - Rhetorical question meaning 'Did you hear music playing underwater? Is it you, Your Majesty?'
  • 老臣前昧死歸訴 - Formal expression meaning 'Your humble servant previously risked his life to return and plead.'
  • 幸蒙陛下天地之施 - Means 'fortunate to receive Your Majesty's boundless grace.'
  • 得全其居 - Means 'to preserve our homes.'
  • 故私相慶樂耳 - Means 'so we privately celebrate in joy.'
  • 可得奏樂否 - Question meaning 'May we play music?'
  • 故赍乐来 - Means 'We have brought the music with us.'
  • 治弦而歌 - Means 'to tune the strings and sing.'
  • 天地德兮垂至仁 - Poetic phrase meaning 'The virtue of heaven and earth, bestowing utmost benevolence.'
  • 愍幽魄兮停斧斤 - Means 'Pitying the souls of the deep, stopping the axes.'
  • 保窟宅兮庇微身 - Means 'Protecting our homes, sheltering our humble selves.'
  • 愿天子兮寿万春 - Means 'May Your Majesty live for ten thousand springs.'
  • 歌声小大无异于人 - Means 'The voice is neither small nor large, just like a person's.'
  • 清彻绕越梁栋 - Means 'The sound is clear and resonates through the beams and pillars.'
  • 又二人鸣管抚节调契声谐 - Means 'And two others play pipes and strike the rhythm, their sounds harmonizing.'
  • 不德不足当雅贶 - Means 'Your Majesty's virtue is too great for me to accept such a gracious gift.'
  • 老翁等并起拜爵 - Means 'The old men all rise to toast.'
  • 各饮数升不醉 - Means 'Each drank several sheng without getting drunk.'
  • 献帝一紫螺壳 - Means 'Presented to the Emperor a purple conch shell.'
  • 中有物状如牛脂 - Means 'Inside is something shaped like cow fat.'

Cultural and Historical Allusions

  • 帝 - Refers to the Emperor, likely the Han Emperor in this context.
  • 老翁 - Refers to an elderly man, likely a representative of the underwater inhabitants.
  • 紫螺壳 - A purple conch shell, often associated with mystical or divine gifts in Chinese literature.
  • 天地德 - Refers to the virtue of heaven and earth, a common theme in Chinese philosophy and literature.
  • 斧斤 - Refers to axes, symbolizing destruction or harm.
  • 窟宅 - Refers to homes or dwellings, often used metaphorically for one's place in life.
  • 微身 - Refers to oneself in a humble or modest way.
  • 万春 - A poetic way of saying 'forever' or 'for a very long time.'

Syntactic Ambiguities

  • 与父同入山 - This phrase can be read as 'went into the mountains with his father' or 'went into the mountains, and his father went with him.'
  • 左右导从四百许 - This phrase can be read as 'with about four hundred attendants on either side' or 'with attendants on both sides, numbering about four hundred.'
  • 前车辎重，马鞭夹道，卤簿如二千石 - This long sequence must be read as a single description of a grand procession, with each element contributing to the overall image.
  • 遥见纵父子，便唤住，就纵手中然火 - This sequence must be read as a series of actions performed by the mysterious figure, emphasizing the sudden and unexpected nature of the encounter.
  • 从此所向如意，田蚕并收，家遂富 - This sequence must be read as a series of outcomes that followed the encounter, emphasizing the positive changes in Zong's life.
  • 项县民姚牛，年十余岁 - This phrase introduces Yao Niu, emphasizing his youth and the tragic circumstances of his father's death.
  • 常卖衣物市刀戟，图欲报仇 - This sequence must be read as a series of actions and intentions, emphasizing Yao Niu's determination to avenge his father.
  • 后在县署前相遇，手刃之于众中 - This sequence must be read as a series of events leading to Yao Niu's act of vengeance, emphasizing the dramatic and public nature of the event.

Idiomatic Expressions

  • 見一大人，身可長丈余，姿顏金色，項有白光 - Describes a figure of great stature and divine appearance. '身可長丈余' means 'the body can be over ten feet tall,' indicating a supernatural or divine being. '姿顏金色' means 'golden complexion,' suggesting a radiant, otherworldly appearance. '項有白光' means 'a white light emanates from the neck,' further emphasizing the divine nature.
  • 坐此床上 - Literally 'sitting on this bed,' but in a religious context, it likely refers to a throne or a seat of honor.
  • 沙門立侍甚眾 - '沙門' refers to Buddhist monks or ascetics. '立侍甚眾' means 'many standing in attendance,' indicating a large number of followers or devotees.
  • 見泰山府君來作禮 - '泰山府君' is the Lord of Mount Tai, a deity in Chinese folk religion. '作禮' means 'to pay respects,' indicating a formal act of reverence.
  • 泰問吏 - '泰' refers to the Lord of Mount Tai, and '問吏' means 'to ask the officials,' indicating a query to the servants or attendants.
  • 此名佛，天上天下，度人之師 - '此名佛' means 'this is called Buddha,' and '天上天下，度人之師' means 'the teacher who guides people in heaven and on earth,' emphasizing the Buddha's universal role.
  • 便聞佛言 - '便聞佛言' means 'then the Buddha spoke,' indicating the beginning of a discourse.
  • 今欲度此恶道中及诸地狱中人，皆令出 - '度此恶道中及诸地狱中人' means 'to liberate those in the evil path and the various hells,' indicating the Buddha's intention to save beings suffering in these realms. '皆令出' means 'to make all of them emerge,' indicating the liberation of these beings.
  • 应时云有万九千人，一时得出地狱 - '应时云有万九千人' means 'at the time, it was said there were 19,000 people,' and '一时得出地狱' means 'all emerged from the hell at once,' indicating the immediate liberation of a large number of beings.
  • 实时见呼十人，当上生天 - '实时见呼十人' means 'immediately, ten people were seen being called,' and '当上生天' means 'to ascend to heaven,' indicating the immediate ascension of these ten individuals.
  • 有车马迎之，升虚空而去 - '有车马迎之' means 'they were met by chariots and horses,' and '升虚空而去' means 'ascended into the void and departed,' indicating a grand and divine departure.
  • 云生来不闻道法，而地狱考治已毕者，当于此城更受変报 - '云生来不闻道法' means 'it was said that those who have not heard the Dharma,' '而地狱考治已毕者' means 'and those whose punishment in hell has ended,' and '当于此城更受変报' means 'will undergo further transformation in this city,' indicating a place for further spiritual development.
  • 入北门，见数千百土屋，中央有瓦屋，广五十余步，下有五百余吏，对录人名作善恶事状，受所変身形之路，各从其所趋去 - '入北门' means 'entering the northern gate,' '见数千百土屋' means 'seeing thousands of earthen houses,' '中央有瓦屋' means 'with a tiled house in the center,' '广五十余步' means 'about 50 paces wide,' '下有五百余吏' means 'with more than 500 officials below,' '对录人名作善恶事状' means 'recording names and deeds of good and evil,' '受所変身形之路' means 'determining the path of transformation into different forms,' and '各从其所趋去' means 'each going their own way,' indicating a place where beings are judged and transformed based on their deeds.
  • 杀生者当作蜉蝣虫，朝生夕死 - '杀生者' means 'those who kill,' '当作蜉蝣虫' means 'will be transformed into mayflies,' and '朝生夕死' means 'living only one day,' indicating the short lifespan of mayflies as a consequence of killing.
  • 若为人，常短命 - '若为人' means 'if they become humans,' and '常短命' means 'they will often have short lives,' indicating a consequence of past actions.
  • 偷盗者作猪羊，身屠，肉偿人 - '偷盗者' means 'those who steal,' '作猪羊' means 'will be transformed into pigs and sheep,' '身屠' means 'their bodies will be slaughtered,' and '肉偿人' means 'their meat will be used to pay off debts,' indicating a cycle of retribution.
  • 淫逸者作鹄鹜蛇身 - '淫逸者' means 'those who indulge in lust,' '作鹄鹜蛇身' means 'will be transformed into cranes, ducks, and snakes,' indicating a transformation into lower forms due to immoral behavior.
  • 恶舌者作鸱鸮鸺鹠恶声，人闻皆咒令死 - '恶舌者' means 'those who speak evil,' '作鸱鸮鸺鹠恶声' means 'will be transformed into owls and screech owls with evil voices,' and '人闻皆咒令死' means 'people will curse them to death when they hear them,' indicating the consequences of spreading harmful words.

Cultural and Historical Allusions

  • 泰山府君 - The Lord of Mount Tai, a deity in Chinese folk religion, often associated with the afterlife and judgment.
  • 真人菩萨 - The Four Truthful Bodhisattvas, important figures in Buddhism who embody wisdom and compassion.
  • 佛 - The Buddha, the central figure in Buddhism, known as the teacher who guides people in heaven and on earth.
  • 地狱 - Hell, a realm of suffering and punishment in Buddhist cosmology.
  • 受変形城 - The City of Transformation, a place where beings undergo further spiritual development after their initial punishment in hell.
  • 蜉蝣 - Mayflies, known for their short lifespan, often used as a metaphor for the fleeting nature of life.
  • 鸱鸮鸺鹠 - Owls and screech owls, often associated with evil and curses in Chinese folklore.

Syntactic Ambiguities

  • 見一大人，身可長丈余，姿顏金色，項有白光，坐此床上 - This long descriptive phrase must be read as a single unit, describing a divine figure.
  • 沙門立侍甚眾 - This phrase describes the presence of many Buddhist monks or ascetics.
  • 四座名‘真人菩萨’ - This phrase identifies the four seats as belonging to the Four Truthful Bodhisattvas.
  • 見泰山府君來作禮，泰問吏 - This sequence describes the Lord of Mount Tai paying respects and asking the officials.
  • 此名佛，天上天下，度人之師 - This phrase identifies the figure as the Buddha and emphasizes his role as a teacher.
  • 便聞佛言，今欲度此恶道中及诸地狱中人，皆令出 - This sequence describes the Buddha speaking and expressing his intention to liberate beings.
  • 应时云有万九千人，一时得出地狱 - This phrase indicates the immediate liberation of a large number of beings.
  • 实时见呼十人，当上生天，有车马迎之，升虚空而去 - This sequence describes the immediate ascension of ten individuals to heaven.
  • 复见一城云纵广二百里，名为‘受変形城’ - This phrase introduces a new city where beings undergo further transformation.
  • 云生来不闻道法，而地狱考治已毕者，当于此城更受変报 - This phrase explains the purpose of the City of Transformation.
  • 入北门，见数千百土屋，中央有瓦屋，广五十余步，下有五百余吏，对录人名作善恶事状，受所変身形之路，各从其所趋去 - This long sequence describes the layout and function of the City of Transformation.
  • 杀生者当作蜉蝣虫，朝生夕死；若为人，常短命 - This sequence describes the consequences of killing.

  • 东北出 - Directional phrase meaning 'go northeast,' indicating the direction of travel.
  • 见一城，方数十里，有满城土屋 - Literally 'see a city, square several tens of miles, has a city full of earthen houses,' describing the size and type of dwellings in the city.
  • 因见未事佛时亡伯、伯母、亡叔、叔母，皆着杻械，衣裳破坏，身体脓血 - Complex sentence describing the vision of deceased relatives (uncle, aunt, uncle, aunt) who are suffering in hell, bound in chains, with torn clothes, and covered in pus and blood.
  • 复前行，见一城，其中有卧铁床上者，烧床正赤 - Complex sentence describing a subsequent vision of a city with people lying on iron beds that are burning red.
  • 凡见十狱，各有楚毒 - Literally 'in total, see ten hells, each with its own punishments,' indicating the variety of torments in the afterlife.
  • 狱名“赤沙”、“黄沙”、“白沙”，如此“七沙” - Describes the names of the hells, specifically mentioning 'Red Sand,' 'Yellow Sand,' 'White Sand,' and so on, up to 'Seven Sands.'
  • 有刀山剑树，抱赤铜柱 - Describes specific tortures, such as knife mountains and sword trees, and holding onto red copper pillars.
  • 于是便还 - Literally 'thus return,' indicating the end of the journey.
  • 复见七八十梁间瓦屋，夹道种槐，云名“福舍”，诸佛弟子住中 - Describes a vision of a place with many tiled houses, lined with willows, called 'Blessed Houses,' where Buddhist disciples live.
  • 福多者上生天，福少者住此舍 - Describes the fate of those with more or less merit, either ascending to heaven or staying in the Blessed Houses.
  • 遥见大殿二十余梁，有二男子、二妇人从殿上来下，是得事佛后亡伯、伯母、亡叔、叔母 - Describes the vision of a grand hall with two men and two women, identified as the deceased relatives who have practiced Buddhism.
  • 须臾，有一道人来，问得：“识我不？”得曰：“不识。”曰：“汝何以不识我？我共汝作佛图主。”于是遂而忆之，还至府君所，即遣前二人送归，忽便苏活也 - Describes the encounter with a monk who reveals his identity and sends the narrator back to life.

Cultural and Historical Allusions

  • 东北 - Northeast, a direction.
  • 城 - City, a place.
  • 土屋 - Earthen house, a type of dwelling.
  • 未事佛时 - Not having practiced Buddhism, a state of religious practice.
  • 亡伯、伯母、亡叔、叔母 - Deceased relatives (uncle, aunt, uncle, aunt).
  • 杻械 - Chains or shackles, a form of punishment.
  • 衣裳 - Clothes, a type of clothing.
  • 身体脓血 - Body covered in pus and blood, a state of suffering.
  • 十狱 - Ten hells, a concept in Buddhist cosmology.
  • 刀山剑树 - Knife mountains and sword trees, specific tortures.
  • 抱赤铜柱 - Holding onto red copper pillars, another form of torture.
  • 福舍 - Blessed Houses, a place of refuge.
  • 诸佛弟子 - Buddhist disciples, followers of Buddhism.
  • 大殿 - Grand hall, a place of importance.
  • 道人 - Monk, a religious figure.
  • 佛图主 - Temple master, a high-ranking Buddhist official.

[121]

Idiomatic Expressions

  • 石长和死，四日苏 - Literal phrase meaning 'Shi Changhe died, and four days later he revived.' Sets the stage for a supernatural narrative.
  • 东南行 - Means 'traveling southeast,' indicating the direction of the journey.
  • 治道 - Literally 'repairing the road,' but in this context, it likely refers to preparing or maintaining a path.
  • 棘刺皆如鹰爪 - Vivid description meaning 'thorns and brambles are all like eagle claws,' emphasizing the sharpness and danger of the environment.
  • 独乐 - Means 'enjoyment alone' or 'solitary pleasure,' highlighting the contrast between the individual's experience and the suffering of others.
  • 梁瓦屋 - Refers to 'houses with beams and tiles,' indicating a well-constructed or traditional dwelling.
  • 阁十余 - Means 'ten or more pavilions,' suggesting a complex or multi-story building.
  • 面辟方三尺 - Describes a face that is three feet wide, emphasizing the size and possibly the importance of the figure.
  • 着皂袍 - Means 'wearing a black robe,' indicating the attire of the figure.
  • 四纵掖 - Describes the sleeves of the robe, possibly meaning 'fourfold sleeves' or 'wide sleeves.'
  • 凭向坐 - Means 'leaning against the wall and sitting,' describing the posture of the figure.
  • 贤者识承不 - Rhetorical question meaning 'Do you recognize Cheng?' indicating familiarity or recognition.
  • 晏然与官家事 - Means 'calmly doing official duties,' suggesting a peaceful and orderly state.

Cultural and Historical Allusions

  • 石长和 - The name of the protagonist, Shi Changhe, who is the subject of the narrative.
  • 佛弟子 - Refers to a follower of Buddhism, indicating the religious context of the story.
  • 冯翊牧孟承 - Refers to Meng Cheng, a historical or legendary figure mentioned in the text.
  • 孟承妻 - Refers to Meng Cheng's wife, who is also mentioned in the narrative.
  • 阁上人 - Refers to a figure on the pavilion, likely a spiritual or authoritative being given the context.

Proper Nouns

  • 石长和 - The protagonist of the narrative.
  • 冯翊牧孟承 - A historical or legendary figure mentioned in the text.
  • 孟承妻 - The wife of Meng Cheng, also mentioned in the narrative.
  • 阁上人 - A figure on the pavilion, likely a spiritual or authoritative being.

[122]

Idiomatic Expressions

  • 妻即开窗向 - Ambiguous phrase; could mean 'the wife immediately opened the window to look out' or 'to greet him'.
  • 遍问其家中儿女大小名字平安不 - Elliptical phrase; means 'she asked about the safety and well-being of all her family members, both young and old'.
  • 还时过此，当因一封书 - Ambiguous phrase; could mean 'when you return, pass by here and leave a letter' or 'stop by and bring a letter'.
  • 亦问家消息 - Means 'she also asked about family news'.
  • 阁上人曰 - Refers to a person in a high position or a deity, possibly a guardian or a spiritual figure.
  • 闻鱼龙超修精进，为信尔不？何所修行？ - Questions about the spiritual progress and practices of '鱼龙', a mythical creature or a person with extraordinary abilities.
  • 长和曰 - Name of the person being questioned in a spiritual or religious setting.
  • 不食鱼肉，酒不经口，恒转尊经，救诸疾痛 - Descriptions of the spiritual practices of '长和', including abstaining from meat and alcohol, constantly reciting sacred texts, and helping those in pain.
  • 阁上人曰 - Response from the person in a high position, suggesting a spiritual or religious context.
  • 所传莫妄 - Means 'what has been transmitted is not false', common in Chinese religious and philosophical texts.
  • 阁上问都录主者 - Refers to the person in a high position and the recorder or registrar, suggesting a bureaucratic or administrative context.
  • 石贤者命尽耶？枉夺其命耶？ - Questions about whether '石贤者' has reached the end of his life or if his life has been unjustly taken, suggesting a spiritual or religious context.
  • 主者报 - Refers to the recorder or registrar, suggesting a bureaucratic or administrative context.
  • 按录余四十年 - Means 'according to the record, he has forty more years left', suggesting a spiritual or religious context.
  • 阁上人敕主者 - Refers to the person in a high position instructing the recorder, suggesting a bureaucratic or administrative context.
  • ■〈车卖〉车一乘，两辟车骑，两吏送石贤者 - Suggests a grand procession, common in Chinese literature and mythology.
  • 须臾，东向便有车骑人从如所差之数 - Suggests a sudden appearance of carriages and escorts, common in Chinese literature and mythology.
  • 长和拜辞，上车而归 - Suggests a formal farewell, common in Chinese literature and mythology.
  • 前所行道边，所在有亭传、吏民、床坐饮食之具 - Suggests a well-prepared journey, common in Chinese literature and mythology.
  • 倏然归家 - Suggests a sudden return, common in Chinese literature and mythology.
  • 前见父母坐其尸边 - Suggests a scene of death and mourning, common in Chinese literature and mythology.
  • 见尸大如牛，闻尸臭 - Suggests a scene of decay, common in Chinese literature and mythology.
  • 不欲入其中，绕尸三匝，长和叹息，当尸头前 - Suggests a scene of grief and reflection, common in Chinese literature and mythology.
  • 见其亡姊于后推之，便踣尸面上，因即苏 - Suggests a supernatural event, common in Chinese literature and mythology.
